campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the fees that businesses pay for their licenses in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented information about the proposed changes to these license tax rates, and the council discussed the matter before voting on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Finance Department presented the annual update on licensing fees
Council will vote on approving new business license tax rates for the fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community feedback on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with introducing the ordinance and plans to vote on officially adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on a new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Specific ordinance details not provided in available agenda information

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting records from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services to improve town infrastructure.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed city's February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other firms
Approved infrastructure improvement projects

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve meeting minutes and discuss several projects affecting the city. Key items included plans to replace an old elevator at a terminal building, decisions about hiring companies to clean up homeless encampments, a deal with Clear Channel for digital billboards on city property, and approval for a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade underway
Encampment Cleanup Services - city seeking proposals from contractors
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel - billboards on city-owned locations
Light the Night Firefighter Memorial Event - District 7 sponsored event getting city funding

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear directly from residents during public comment time. The main focus was deciding which neighborhood projects and community groups would receive funding for the 2026-27 budget year, along with reviewing how often council members attended neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget
Public comment period for residents to speak to council
Council attendance at neighborhood meetings
Meeting minutes approval

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, focused on interviewing candidates for open positions on various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and comment both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment period
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss buying or selling a property on Finch Avenue with the local school district, with the city manager and attorney leading negotiations on price and payment terms. The council also reviewed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and discussed plans for getting grant funding for it in the future.
keyTopics: Land deal negotiations with Cupertino Union School District for 10480 Finch Avenue property
Price and payment terms being worked out between city and school district
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss putting up large promotional signs around Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup games coming to the area. The council also needed to approve a new contract with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one item was withdrawn before discussion.
keyTopics: Large promotional signs approved for Levi's Stadium during 2026 FIFA World Cup
New 3-year contract finalized with Santa Clara Firefighters union
Temporary signage will be allowed on building surfaces near the stadium
One agenda item withdrawn by city staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve the city's spending plans for the next five years and discuss the budget for 2026-27. They also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the City Council runs its meetings.
keyTopics: 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031) approved
2026-27 operating budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council procedures and protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including routine administrative matters and a major development proposal. The main focus was a public hearing on a plan to subdivide a vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine single-family homes and seven smaller accessory dwelling units, which would require creating a new private street to access them.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre lot at 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 homes plus 7 accessory units
Creation of new private street to access the residences
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Director's report on city operations and meeting schedule
